summary: Backend-only pytest run for AI Criminal Case Management System. 29/29 tests PASSED covering auth (register/login/me/duplicate/bad password), FIR create/list/detail/status-update with role enforcement, suspects RBAC, evidence upload+SHA256 verify+tampering detection+download, forensic records, blockchain chaining/integrity, analytics stats, activity logs, admin user CRUD (list/update/delete), crime report and citizen-cannot-access enforcement on privileged endpoints. Found and fixed one CRITICAL 500 bug in /api/auth/register caused by Mongo _id leaking into response.

critical.issue: Returned HTTP 500 'ObjectId is not iterable'. Root cause: db.users.insert_one(user_doc) mutates user_doc to add ObjectId _id; the subsequent user_out dict-comprehension excluded only 'password_hash' and kept '_id', making FastAPI's jsonable_encoder fail. FIXED by excluding '_id' as well (server.py line 208).

minor.issue: app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, ...) is added AFTER app.include_router(api). In Starlette this still works because middleware is applied to the ASGI stack at startup, but convention is to add middleware before including routers. Functionally OK.
Permits 'police' role in addition to admin (see line 448). Spec says 'admin only' — verify if this is intended. Non-blocking.
Admin is always allowed (bypass baked into checker). This is acceptable but should be documented.

action_items: Review /api/activity-logs access policy: currently admin+police, spec says admin only.
Consider moving CORSMiddleware registration before include_router for best practice (no current regression).
Optional: add pydantic response_model to register/login/me to prevent future ObjectId leaks.
critical_code_review_comments: Login endpoint already pops _id correctly — apply same pattern via a shared user_serializer() helper to avoid repetition.

append_block serializes `data` using str(dict) which is unstable ordering pre-3.7; Python 3.11 preserves insertion order so OK, but using json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True) would be more deterministic for chain integrity.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout) — not implemented.
Evidence verify endpoint is callable by any authenticated user including citizens for any evidence_id — consider scoping to case owner/police/forensic/admin.
No Content-Type validation / max file size on /api/evidence upload.
test_credentials: Admin admin@cjs.gov / Admin@123 verified working. Ephemeral citizen/police/forensic users created per-run via /api/auth/register with timestamped emails.
seed_data_creation: None persisted intentionally; tests create ephemeral TEST_ data (one FIR, one suspect, one evidence, one forensic record, one tampered-file). Dedicated cleanup not performed since data is safely prefixed/uniquely timestamped.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API fully green. Next agent can focus on frontend UI + end-to-end integration. Reuse /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for regression. Evidence tampering test writes extra bytes to /app/backend/uploads/<file>; uploads dir remains populated with test artifacts.
rca of the issue: POST /api/auth/register returned 500. RCA: Motor's insert_one mutates the provided dict by assigning the generated ObjectId to key '_id'. The handler built user_out via dict-comprehension filtering only 'password_hash', so '_id' (ObjectId) remained and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder raised TypeError('ObjectId is not iterable'). Reproduction: curl -X POST /api/auth/register with fresh email → 500. Mitigation: also exclude '_id' from user_out (applied). Long-term: add a response_model or shared serializer.
